meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop chasing clients without a track record. Learn to build a high-converting SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code to prove your expertise fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by building a functional, high-value SaaS MVP rather than a static site. By leveraging a SaaS starter kit, you compress months of development into hours. This generates a live, revenue-ready product that serves as undeniable proof of your technical authority and commercial execution capability.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, the silence of a non-existent client list echoing in your inbox. Every potential lead asks for a portfolio you don't have, creating a brutal feedback loop: you can't get clients without work, and you can't get work without clients. It’s the classic 'chicken and egg' dilemma that kills most promising founders before they ever launch.

But the rules of the game have shifted. In the age of AI-assisted engineering, your portfolio isn't a gallery of old designs—it’s a live, shipping SaaS product. By deploying a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you bypass the junior-level 'projects' and build a revenue-generating machine. Let’s turn that empty portfolio into a showcase of high-end, scalable architecture.

steps.title: Define Your MVP Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, define the core value proposition. Use Antigravity to scaffold a robust Next.js/Supabase architecture that handles authentication and payments out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Generate a scaffold for a SaaS starter kit using Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Include pre-configured Stripe integration and a clean dashboard layout.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Revenue Hooks
steps.description: A portfolio project without revenue features is just a hobby. Configure webhooks to handle subscription events and demonstrate that you understand the SaaS business model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler for Next.js that updates user subscription status in the Supabase database. Ensure error handling for failed payments.

steps.title: Build High-Conversion UI Components
steps.description: Use Claude Code to rapidly iterate on UI components that prioritize conversion. Your portfolio needs to look like a million-dollar product, not a template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting landing page hero section and a pricing table component using Radix UI and Tailwind. Focus on clarity and CTA placement.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Show potential clients you handle data integrity. Set up automated migrations so your database schema evolves safely as you add new features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a migration script for a multi-tenant SaaS database schema in Supabase, ensuring row-level security (RLS) is enabled for all tables.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: A local project is invisible. Deploy your starter kit to Vercel or Railway to demonstrate that you can manage the full CI/CD lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that automates testing and deployment to Vercel on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from Day 1; it shows enterprise-level thinking that clients will pay a premium for.
Include a 'Usage Analytics' dashboard in your starter kit—showing you understand data-driven growth is a massive competitive advantage.
Don't waste time on custom CSS; use component libraries like Shadcn UI to maintain a consistent, high-end design language instantly.
Implement 'Feature Flags' early, allowing you to toggle features for beta users without redeploying your entire application.
Focus on SEO-ready metadata and Open Graph images in your starter kit; it proves you understand the 'build for distribution' mindset.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, don't just leave it on GitHub. Write a case study detailing the technical challenges you solved, share it on X/Twitter, and start reaching out to founders with a 'I built this for your niche' pitch.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a professional. You have the tools to build a product that proves your value, generates revenue, and secures your next client. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
